behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum for A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/range resolver/function dispatcher/value store handoff, not one isolated function. No real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text only checks arithmetic/range evaluation correctness, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-process workbook with A1..A10 seeded, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)`, assert computed value matches expected sum via the normal engine path (parse -> reference resolution -> function eval).

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another formula cell (e.g., `=B1+1`, `B1=A1*2`) yields correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundary between dependency resolution and formula evaluation across cells. Unit is too low because single-function tests can miss cross-cell cascade wiring faults. No real infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1, define B1 as `=A1*2`, define C1 as `=B1+1`, evaluate C1 through the engine, assert final value and that intermediate dependency evaluation occurred correctly.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1->B1->A1) is detected and returns circular reference error.
behaviors.justification: Detection must work in live dependency graph/evaluator interaction; isolated cycle-check helper tests are insufficient for real engine loop prevention. No OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Construct workbook with A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`, run evaluation via normal engine entrypoint, assert circular-reference error is returned and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily function semantics (lookup and error contract) and can fail within the VLOOKUP implementation boundary without multi-module wiring. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text does not require validating dependency orchestration, only VLOOKUP result semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call VLOOKUP function implementation with deterministic table/range fixtures: one with a matching key and one missing key; assert returned value and not-found error code.

behaviors.justification: Core failure is expression evaluation logic for nested conditionals, which is testable at local evaluator/function boundary without external wiring. Rejection-gate: Integration considered, but behavior text targets conditional semantics, not module handoff/state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` against multiple A1 values in a local evaluator test and assert branch outputs.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: This is local function policy (string join + empty-cell handling). No required cross-module boundary, real infra, or AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke CONCATENATE implementation with fixtures including empty cells and separators; assert output omits empties rather than inserting blank segments.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Requires workbook/sheet namespace resolution plus evaluator wiring; single-function tests can miss cross-sheet binding failures. Rejection-gate: System considered due filesystem in product scope, but behavior text is in-memory reference resolution only.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1, evaluate Sheet1 formula `=Sheet2!A1+1` through engine, assert correct resolved value.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell changes, dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff across dependency graph, invalidation, and evaluator modules; unit tests of one function miss ordering bugs. No real OS/network/subprocess required and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency chain/diamond, mutate upstream cell, trigger recalculation, assert updated values and evaluation order consistent with topological dependencies.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is history manager + formula storage + recalculation interaction; not a single local function. Rejection-gate: System considered, but behavior text does not require OS/process behavior, only in-engine state restoration.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that affects dependents, compute, invoke undo, then assert formula source and all impacted dependent values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save to disk and reload yields identical formulas/values with no floating-point precision change.
behaviors.justification: Minimum real-failure boundary is serializer/deserializer + workbook model round-trip; local filesystem use is sufficient. Rejection-gate: System considered due disk wording, but behavior text checks binary round-trip fidelity/precision, not OS-specific failure modes (locking, permissions, watcher events, subprocess interaction).
behaviors.test_description: Write workbook with representative formulas and edge-case floating-point values to a temp file via production save path, reload via production load path, assert formula text equality and numeric equivalence at required precision.
